claim.content:
Given a computed band-structure energy eigenvalue field E_n(k) over the Brillouin zone for YbRh2Si2, the numerical algorithm used to identify extremal Fermi-surface cross-sectional areas and convert them to predicted de Haas–van Alphen frequencies via the Onsager relation F = (\hbar/2\pi e) A_ext yields accurate predictions of the angular dependence F(θ) for each Fermi-surface sheet provided the underlying band energies are sufficiently smooth and the k-space discretization resolves the extremal orbits; the algorithm's accuracy may be degraded if bands are extremely flat or if multiple nearly-degenerate sheets exist within the discretization error.
      Rourke P M C and Julian S R Numerical extraction of de Haas-van Alphen frequencies from calculated band energies (Preprint arXiv:0803.1895)
